title: Tamper-evident command evidence item (v2)
description: Canonical shape for ONE recorded command in a QA layer's command_evidence[]. v2 replaces the v1 fabricatable inline `stdout` STRING with a path + SHA256 of the raw captured output, plus a named-parser provenance chain (parser + parser_input_sha256 + parsed_metrics) so a downstream gate can RE-READ the raw file, RE-HASH it, and RE-PARSE it instead of trusting model-typed numbers. Produced by `qa-sdk evidence.run` (the SDK fills every field; the model never types a metric). NOTE: verdict-validator.js has no $ref support, so layer schemas INLINE this item shape (do not $ref it); this file is the canonical definition + the parser/lint source of truth. Legacy v1 items (cmd+exit_code only) remain readable — qa-recompute-gate treats an item WITHOUT stdout_sha256 as UNVERIFIABLE (cannot confirm its numbers), never as verified.

command.description: The literal command line that ran (verbatim, for audit).

exit_code.description: Exit status of the COMMAND (PIPESTATUS[0]), not the tee/pipe.

stdout_path.description: Relative path to the raw captured stdout file under .qa/runs/<tag>/raw/.

stdout_sha256.description: SHA256 of the raw stdout file BYTES (newlines NOT normalized).

artifact_path.description: Optional structured parser input (junit.xml, coverage-summary.json, k6 summary.json…).

parser.description: Named deterministic parser that produced parsed_metrics, e.g. qa-parse-tsc@1.

parser_input.description: Which captured file the parser consumed.

parser_input_sha256.description: SHA256 of the exact bytes fed to the parser (== artifact_sha256 when parser_input=artifact, else == stdout/stderr_sha256). The gate re-derives and compares.

parse_status.description: OK = parser ran clean and produced parsed_metrics (parser then required). SKIPPED = no parser requested (the raw stdout is still hashed + re-verifiable; no metric derived — valid for setup/non-metric commands). PARSER_FAILED / INPUT_MISSING on a selected GATING layer is treated as BLOCK by the recompute gate.

parsed_metrics.description: SDK/parser-DERIVED metrics (line_pct, tsc_errors, lcp_ms, …). The gate re-runs the parser over parser_input and deep-compares; a hand-edited value mismatches and BLOCKs.

git_head.description: G2 binding: `git rev-parse HEAD` at capture time — ties evidence to a commit.

git_dirty_sha256.description: G2 binding: SHA256 of `git status --porcelain` — proves whether the tree was clean. The gate cross-checks this against the release commit to catch stash/edit tricks (audit G2).
